records.title: The conceptual design of the high-efficiency 400 kW solid-state power station at 352 MHz for the European spallation source

records.notes: DOI now confirmed (previously blank). Directly related to the ESS envelope-tracking amplifier work in L05-007 -- distinct from the unrelated same-title-journal L05-051 (see that record's correction note). Author list not itemized via the direct Crossref fetch this session (only title/container/year returned); geography (Sweden) reflects ESS host-site location, not independently fetched author affiliation.

records.title: SEMI F47-0706 (Reapproved 0812)E -- Specification for Semiconductor Processing Equipment Voltage Sag Immunity
records.authors_or_org: SEMI (Semiconductor Equipment and Materials International)
records.claim_supported: SEMI F47 establishes minimum voltage-sag-immunity requirements for semiconductor processing/metrology/test equipment: tolerate sags to 50% of nominal voltage for up to 200 ms, 70% for up to 0.5 s, and 80% for up to 1.0 s. Current revision F47-0706 (reapproved 0812)E; member price $286, non-member $380.
records.notes: Directly relevant to L06's subfab-power-quality niche -- this is the specific standard that codifies why RF/plasma-tool power electronics must tolerate facility-level voltage sags, a real engineering constraint vendors design against. | Directly relevant to L06's subfab-power-quality niche.

records.notes: Directly relevant to the gas-delivery/MFC technical bottleneck identified in academic source L06-026 and the NIST MFC workshop lead (L06-038). | Directly relevant to the gas-delivery/MFC technical bottleneck identified in academic sources L06-026 (Mechatronics 2025).

records.title: Semiconductors: Information on Projects Funded to Strengthen U.S. Supply Chain
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO)
records.claim_supported: Official GAO report (GAO-26-107882) finds that, as of July 2025, Commerce had distributed $30.9 billion in direct CHIPS Act funding plus $5.5 billion in loans across 40 projects benefiting 19 companies; roughly 40% of funding targets advanced logic chips for AI, with the US share of global leading-edge chip production projected to rise from 0% (2022) to 20% (2030); only 1 of 40 projects (an Arizona leading-edge logic fab) had achieved completion certification as of June 2025, with 24 of 161 planned milestones reported complete; Commerce negotiated profit-sharing/upside-sharing agreements with 27 of 40 projects.
records.notes: WebFetch retrieved the PDF binary successfully but could not extract readable text this session (compressed content stream); no specific claim beyond the report's existence and general subject (per gao.gov/products landing page description) should be treated as confirmed. Report does not itemize L06-specific subsystem-vendor dollar figures, but confirms the program scale and audit status relevant to CHIPS-driven equipment demand context.

records.title: Report from the Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) Lithography Working Group Meeting: Current State, Needs, and Path Forward (NIST Special Publication 1500-208)
records.authors_or_org: Rasmussen, Elizabeth; Wilthan, Boris; Simonds, Brian (National Institute of Standards and Technology)
records.claim_supported: Documents an April 25 2023 hybrid NIST working-group meeting (Boulder, CO) bringing together industry and NIST researchers on EUV lithography R&D and manufacturing; the report outlines the current state of the science, challenges, needs, and opportunities for accelerated EUVL innovation, and where NIST metrology expertise could assist industry. The abstract explicitly notes the report is a discussion starting point, not a complete industry census.
records.notes: WebFetch retrieved the PDF but could not extract readable body text (compressed content stream) -- existence and official NIST identity confirmed via the nvlpubs.nist.gov domain and file naming convention, but no specific quoted finding should be used until a verifier re-extracts the text. | Year corrected from the scout's 2020 to 2023 (both meeting date and publication date). Title corrected to NIST's actual title ('Working Group Meeting', not 'Workshop'). No EUV source-power-specific figures should be treated as confirmed since the PDF body text itself was not extracted.

records.title: Advanced Energy (AEIS) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
records.authors_or_org: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc. (NASDAQ: AEIS); transcribed by The Motley Fool
records.claim_supported: Q4 2025 revenue $489 million; full-year 2025 revenue $1.8 billion (+21% YoY); semiconductor segment revenue $840 million full-year (+6% YoY), $212 million in Q4 (+8% sequential). CEO Steve Kelley: 'broad acceptance of EVOS, Everest, and NavX across a broad customer set... customers are encountering problems as they go below two nanometers.' CFO Paul Oldham: new RF/plasma-power products reaching 'double-digit millions of revenue,' still 'largely in early pilot or early production stage.'
records.notes: Directly fetched, quotes attributed to named executives. Third-party-transcribed (not the raw SEC 8-K exhibit) -- classified T2 rather than T1 audited filing, though content is a verbatim record of an official investor call. | Third-party-transcribed (not the raw SEC 8-K exhibit) -- T2. Revenue figures here corroborate the accepted L06-042/043 China-supplier data by establishing the incumbent baseline these Chinese entrants are displacing.

records.title: Axcelis Announces Shipment of Purion H200 SiC Power Series Implanter to Wolfspeed
records.authors_or_org: Axcelis Technologies, Inc. / Wolfspeed, Inc.
records.claim_supported: Axcelis shipped a Purion H200 SiC ion implant evaluation system to Wolfspeed on 2023-07-26 to support production of SiC power devices for EV applications, in the context of Wolfspeed's $6.5 billion capacity-expansion effort. Axcelis CEO Dr. Russell Low: 'The power device market continues to grow rapidly and is a key driver of our growth globally.'
records.notes: Directly fetched joint Axcelis/Wolfspeed press release -- concrete, dated, named-buyer high-voltage-implanter-module demand evidence tied to the SiC power-semiconductor capex cycle. | Concrete, dated, named-buyer high-voltage-implanter-module demand evidence tied to the SiC power-semiconductor capex cycle.

records.title: Shenzhen CSL Vacuum Science and Technology (Hengyunchang) IPO: customer concentration and financial disclosure
records.authors_or_org: Shenzhen CSL Vacuum Science and Technology Co., Ltd. (Hengyunchang, SSE STAR Market 688785); reported by Caixin/Cailianshe (CLS)
records.claim_supported: Hengyunchang's IPO prospectus discloses fundraising of RMB 1.55 billion for a Shenyang semiconductor RF-power-system industrialization project and a core-components smart-manufacturing base; top customer/shareholder Piotech (tuo jing ke ji) accounted for 45.23% of revenue (2022), 58.16% (2023), 63.13% (2024); top five customers together 90.62% of 2024 revenue. Named customers: AMEC, NAURA, Wei Dao Nano, ACM Shanghai. 2024 revenue reported as RMB 5.41 yi yuan (~RMB 541 million); net profit RMB 1.43 yi yuan (~RMB 143 million). Ranked #1 among domestic (China) plasma RF-power-system manufacturers in 2023.
records.notes: This is financial-media reporting on Hengyunchang's SSE STAR Market prospectus/audited IPO financials, not the raw SSE-filed prospectus PDF itself (which exists at static.sse.com.cn but was not directly fetched this session). Figures are internally consistent with the company's own IPO-prospectus-derived numbers reported across multiple independent outlets (cls.cn, eastmoney, 21jingji) this session -- treated as T1 company-filing-derived data with the sourcing caveat noted for the verifier. Chinese-language primary demand evidence -- Piotech, a named shareholder-customer, being 45-63% of revenue is itself a notable concentration/conflict-of-interest risk flagged for the red-team phase. | TIER DOWNGRADED from the scout's T1 to T2 for honesty: this is financial-media reporting on Hengyunchang's SSE STAR Market prospectus, not the raw SSE-filed prospectus PDF itself (static.sse.com.cn was not directly fetched this or the prior session). Figures are internally consistent across multiple independent outlets. Piotech being 45-63% of revenue as a named shareholder-customer is a concentration/conflict-of-interest risk flagged for red-team.

records.title: Hengyunchang lists on SSE STAR Market, shares surge over 300%
records.authors_or_org: Cailianshe (CLS) financial news; reporting on Shenzhen CSL Vacuum Science and Technology Co., Ltd.
records.claim_supported: Hengyunchang listed on the SSE STAR Market on 2026-01-28, raising RMB 1.469 billion; first-day close +302.8%. Company makes plasma RF power-supply systems for semiconductor manufacturing, ending the 'decades-long monopoly' of US suppliers MKS and Advanced Energy in the segment; supplies domestic equipment makers including Piotech, Sineo Semiconductor, NAURA, and partners with chipmakers SMIC and Yangtze Memory Technologies (YMTC).
records.notes: News reporting (not the primary filing document) on the listing-day event -- classified T2. Complements L06-042 (financial/customer detail) with listing-day market reaction and a broader named-customer list (Sineo, SMIC, YMTC in addition to Piotech/AMEC/NAURA/ACM). | News reporting on the listing-day event -- T2. Complements L06-042 with a broader named-customer list.

records.title: Sichuan Injet Electric (Yingjie Dianqi) 2024 Annual Report -- semiconductor RF power segment
records.authors_or_org: Sichuan Injet Electric Co., Ltd. (SZSE: 300820); reported by Tencent News and Caizhongshe
records.claim_supported: Sichuan Injet Electric's FY2024 total operating revenue was RMB 1.78 billion (+0.6% YoY) and net profit attributable to parent RMB 323 million (-25.2% YoY), independently confirmed via two directly fetched financial-media sources this session (Tencent News, Caizhongshe), both matching each other and the scout's original top-line figures exactly. The company's semiconductor/electronic-materials segment is described qualitatively as a growth driver offsetting a photovoltaic-segment downturn.
records.notes: Not directly fetched this session (identified via WebSearch synthesis of the Eastmoney article); figures are from the company's mandatory SZSE-listed annual report (an audited filing) as reported by financial media. Chinese-language primary demand evidence showing a second, independent domestic RF-power supplier (distinct from Hengyunchang) with real, growing PECVD RF-power order volume. | TIER DOWNGRADED from the scout's T1 to T2 (secondary financial-media reporting, not the primary SZSE annual-report PDF, which returned unreadable/binary content on two separate direct-fetch attempts this session). The scout's specific segment-revenue figure (RMB 185.2154 million / 23.24% of revenue) and gross-margin figure (38.50%) could NOT be reproduced from any source fetched this session (a WebSearch-derived alternate figure of RMB 350.6 million / 19.69% also surfaced but was not independently fetch-confirmed) -- both segment-level figures are dropped from the claim as unconfirmed; only the corroborated top-line revenue/profit figures are retained.

records.title: A*STAR and Applied Materials Announce New Joint Lab for Semiconductor Equipment and Local Supply Chain Development
records.authors_or_org: Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB) / Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR)
records.claim_supported: On 2024-09-05, A*STAR and Applied Materials announced the Applied Materials-A*STAR Joint Lab for Applied Process Equipment Accelerator (APEX), involving five A*STAR institutes, to develop semiconductor-equipment components/methods targeting ICAPS markets and to build local SME supply-chain capability. Investment amount not disclosed.
records.notes: Directly fetched official Singapore government-agency (EDB) release -- a real, dated government-backed equipment/supply-chain development trigger for Singapore, though the investment amount is not disclosed (unlike the 2015 predecessor joint lab, which was S$150 million per a separate news search) -- flagged so the verifier does not conflate the two. | Real, dated government-backed equipment/supply-chain development trigger for Singapore.

records.title: AMEC 2024 revenue exceeds RMB 9 billion, etch equipment leads market
records.authors_or_org: Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment Inc. China (AMEC, SSE STAR Market 688012); reported by Securities Times (stcn.com)
records.claim_supported: AMEC's 2024 operating revenue reached approximately RMB 9.065 billion (+44.73% YoY); etch equipment (CCP and ICP plasma etch tools) generated approximately RMB 7.277 billion (+54.73% YoY); R&D spending reached RMB 2.45 billion (+94.3% YoY, ~27% of revenue). AMEC states its etch tools cover over 95% of China's domestic etch-application requirements, with production on 5nm-and-below lines.
records.notes: News reporting quoting AMEC's official 2024 performance disclosure (not the raw SSE-filed annual report itself) -- classified T2 on the sourcing caveat, though the underlying figures are from a mandatory listed-company disclosure. AMEC is China's largest domestic etch-tool maker and a major buyer of L06 subsystems (RF power, ESCs, gas delivery) from suppliers like Hengyunchang (L06-042/043) -- this record anchors the scale of that downstream demand. | AMEC is China's largest domestic etch-tool maker and a major buyer of L06 subsystems (RF power, ESCs, gas delivery) from suppliers like Hengyunchang (L06-042/043).

records.title: Comet Group -- RF Power (product/business overview)
records.authors_or_org: Comet Holding AG / Comet Plasma Control Technologies (Switzerland)
records.claim_supported: Comet's RF Power Delivery System -- impedance matching networks, RF generators, and vacuum capacitors -- controls plasma processing of silicon wafers and glass substrates; Comet's Synertia platform (launched July 2022) is positioned as an integrated real-time RF power delivery platform for multi-layer next-generation memory and atomic-scale plasma processes.
records.notes: Directly fetched but this specific page did not contain the $1.6 billion RF-power-subsystem market-size / ~5% CAGR figure that appeared in a separate search-engine summary of Comet's annual report materials -- that figure is NOT included here to avoid citing an unconfirmed number; only claims actually present on the fetched page are used. A follow-up scout/verifier should locate the exact Comet annual-report page containing the market-size figure and log it separately with a direct fetch. | Vendor claim, not independent validation. A separate $1.6B RF-power-subsystem market-size figure that appeared in search summaries was correctly excluded since it was not present on the actually-fetched page.

records.title: Kyosan Electric Manufacturing -- RF Generator products
records.authors_or_org: Kyosan Electric Manufacturing Co., Ltd. (Japan)
records.claim_supported: Kyosan offers two catalogued RF generator models for plasma processing: HFK15TP (400 kHz, 1.50 kW, MOS-FET) and RFK35TS (13.56 MHz, 3.5 kW, MOS-FET, water-cooled); both rated at harmonic distortion below -45 dB/dBc; other VHF-band frequencies available on request.
records.notes: Japanese-language primary vendor source with real technical specifications (frequency, power rating, cooling method) -- satisfies the local-language Japan requirement. No pricing disclosed. Company also holds patents in Japan/Germany/US for its Class-D RF amplifier technology per company Wikipedia/product pages (not independently verified this session). | Japanese-language primary vendor source with real technical specifications -- satisfies the local-language Japan requirement.

records.title: China's Domestic Chip Equipment Adoption Beats 2025 Target at 35%, Led by NAURA, AMEC
records.authors_or_org: TrendForce
records.claim_supported: China's domestically manufactured semiconductor equipment share rose from 25% (2024) to 35% (2025), exceeding the 30% target; by category, etching/thin-film deposition >40%, metrology 25%, lithography 18%. NAURA, AMEC, Piotech, and ACM Research achievements named.
records.notes: TrendForce is a Taiwan-headquartered semiconductor research/trade-press firm reporting on Chinese mainland equipment localization -- tagged with both CN and TW geography. Notably conflicts with L06-052's figures (25%->35% here vs. 4%->13%->21% there) for what appears to be a differently scoped metric -- flagged as an unresolved contradiction for the lane brief. | CONFLICT FLAG (unresolved, not averaged): directly contradicts L06-052's 4%->13%->21% trajectory for a nominally similar 'China equipment localization rate' metric. Different scope/methodology likely; both records retained and the conflict must be shown, not reconciled, in the lane brief.

records.title: China's Semiconductor Equipment Localization Rate Jumps to 21%: Etching Tools Enter TSMC Lines, Lithography Remains the Final Hurdle
records.authors_or_org: BigGo Finance (Tianxia Gongchang / faxiangongchang.com research network)
records.claim_supported: China's overall semiconductor equipment localization progressed roughly 4% (2018) -> 13% -> 21% (2025). By category: etching 31%, thin-film deposition ~27%, cleaning >50% in sub-segments; lithography/coating-developing/metrology-inspection below 10%. AMEC tools deployed on TSMC's 5nm lines; SMEE delivered its first 28nm immersion lithography machine in 2025; NAURA H1 2025 revenue ~$2.2 billion.
records.notes: Directly conflicts with L06-051's 25%->35% figure for what is nominally the same 'China equipment localization rate' metric -- an unresolved contradiction (likely different scope/methodology between the two research sources) to be flagged explicitly in the lane brief and not averaged. | CONFLICT FLAG (unresolved, not averaged): directly contradicts L06-051's 25%->35% figure for the same nominal metric. Both retained; conflict to be shown explicitly in the lane brief.

records.title: Mass Flow Controller (MFC) For Semiconductor Market Size, Share & Trends, 2035
records.authors_or_org: Global Growth Insights (market-research consultancy)
records.claim_supported: Consultancy estimate: global mass flow controller market for semiconductor applications valued at USD 1.05 billion in 2025, projected to reach USD 2 billion by 2035 (CAGR 6.5%, 2026-2035); report claims '85+ data points across 18 countries' and that ~70% of semiconductor fabs rely on MFC technology (unsourced within the report itself). Top vendors by share: HORIBA (17%), MKS Instruments (14%), plus Fujikin, Sevenstar, Hitachi Metals, Pivotal Systems, Bronkhorst, Brooks, Sensirion, and others.
records.notes: Per SOURCE_STANDARDS, unaudited consultancy market-size estimates are T3/context-only and cannot establish TAM alone. Not directly fetched (landing page only, no bottom-up methodology visible); a competing Fortune Business Insights MFC market report was also found in search but not logged separately to avoid citing two unreconciled consultancy numbers as if independent. | Unaudited single-source consultancy estimate -- T3/context-only per SOURCE_STANDARDS; cannot establish TAM alone and is not independently triangulated against a second consultancy figure in this record.

records.title: Two major demand drivers boost RF power-supply market growth; domestic manufacturers gaining strength
records.authors_or_org: Yicai (Diyi Caijing) financial media
records.claim_supported: China's semiconductor RF power-supply market was valued at approximately US$0.97 billion in 2022 (13.31% of the global market), with a low domestic-localization rate and the global market dominated by two US manufacturers (MKS, Advanced Energy) plus Japanese/German competitors. Hengyunchang has a strategic-cooperation agreement with Tuojing Technology, with some RF-power products passing verification/entering mass production. Injet Electric has reportedly achieved 80-90% domestic substitution on 6-inch/8-inch fab lines over five years, targeting 50% coverage on 12-inch lines within five years. Shanghai Yianen Semiconductor, which began in RF-generator repair services, claims 'nearly 95% of wafer fabs' as customers.
records.notes: Not directly fetched (identified via WebSearch synthesis only) -- the specific <12% localization figure should be re-confirmed by direct fetch before load-bearing use, since it is a striking number that sits alongside the higher etch/deposition-tool-level localization figures in L06-051/052 (illustrating that component-level localization lags whole-tool-level localization significantly). | CORRECTION: the actually-fetched article content does not support the scout's original claim of a '<12% localization rate' figure (that specific figure could not be reproduced this session and is dropped); the article's real claims (market size, share, and named-company substitution rates) are used instead. This materially changes and strengthens the record: Injet Electric's claimed 80-90% domestic substitution on legacy nodes is a notable, more specific demand data point than the dropped figure.

records.title: Wonik IPS -- Business Overview
records.authors_or_org: Wonik IPS Co., Ltd. (Korea, KOSDAQ: 240810)
records.claim_supported: Wonik IPS states it develops advanced circuit-fabrication process equipment, primarily thin-film deposition equipment; achieved mass production of 3D NAND Flash mold-process equipment in 2014 and entered the 10nm-class High-K DRAM market in 2018. Over 60% of Wonik IPS revenue comes from Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix.
records.notes: Korean-language primary company source; page confirms deposition-equipment business but does not itemize RF power supply or gas-cabinet products specifically (those appear on deeper product pages not fetched this session) -- included as a Korean-language primary vendor source for the L06 equipment-maker ecosystem (Samsung/SK Hynix-anchored), with the specificity limitation noted honestly. | Korean-language primary company source; included as a Korean-language primary vendor source for the L06 equipment-maker ecosystem.

records.title: Nonevaporable getter coating chambers for extreme high vacuum
records.authors_or_org: Marcy L. Stutzman, Philip A. Adderley, Md Abdullah A. Mamun, Matt Poelker; Journal of Vacuum Science & Technology A (AIP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: NEG (Ti-Zr-V) coating turns an accelerator beam-pipe chamber into a distributed pump, reducing thermal and beam-stimulated outgassing enough to reach extreme high vacuum without discrete pumps; work by Jefferson Lab's photoinjector group (Stutzman/Adderley/Mamun/Poelker).
records.notes: Geography corrected from CH (CERN guess) to US: authors are Jefferson Lab (Newport News, VA), not CERN. This is the same research group as L07-002. An arXiv preprint mirror (1803.07056) is discovery-only; this AIP JVST A record is the peer-reviewed final.

records.title: A road map to extreme high vacuum
records.authors_or_org: P. Adderley, G. Myneni; Journal of Physics: Conference Series (IOP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: Outlines a practical path (helium leak detection, cryosorption, low-cost diffusion barriers, clean assembly) to extend vacuum systems from UHV into the XHV regime, from the Jefferson Lab photoinjector/SRF vacuum group.
records.notes: Perspective/roadmap paper by a named authority (Redhead, National Research Council Canada); treated as T2 review rather than primary data paper. A separate 1999 Vacuum-journal version of similar title exists (see L07-003 for a distinct related Redhead piece) — treated as separate works, not merged, pending verifier check for duplication. | MAJOR CORRECTION: raw ledger misattributed this paper to P. A. Redhead (National Research Council Canada) and set geography CA. Crossref confirms the actual authors are P. Adderley and G. Myneni (Jefferson Lab, US) -- the same first author as L07-001. Authors and geography corrected accordingly. JPCS is a conference-proceedings journal with lighter editorial review than a full journal, so tier kept at T2 per source standards.

records.title: My never-ending story towards XHV pressure measurements
records.authors_or_org: Fumio Watanabe; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: First-person historical review of total-pressure gauge development aimed at overcoming the X-ray limit and ESD-ion limit in XHV pressure measurement.
records.notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: raw ledger misattributed this paper to P. A. Redhead and set geography CA (same error pattern as L07-002). Crossref confirms the actual author is Fumio Watanabe. Geography changed to JP as a tentative inference from the author's name only (not an independently confirmed affiliation) -- flagged for further confirmation. A distinct genuine Redhead paper, 'UHV and XHV pressure measurements' (Vacuum, 1993, DOI 10.1016/0042-207x(93)90095-r), was surfaced during this verification but is not part of the raw ledger and is out of scope to add.

records.title: The development of vacuum measurements down to extremely high vacuum – XHV
records.authors_or_org: Anita Calcatelli; Measurement (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Surveys ionization-gauge development (including CNT-cathode gauges) for pressure measurement below the UHV/XHV boundary and the remaining open problems; a 107-reference review.
records.notes: Author identified (was 'unknown'); geography corrected from US placeholder to IT (Anita Calcatelli, Istituto Nazionale di Ricerca Metrologica, Italy).

records.title: Applications of Vacuum Measurement Technology in China's Space Programs
records.authors_or_org: Detian Li, Yongjun Wang, Huzhong Zhang, Zhenhua Xi, Gang Li; Space: Science & Technology (AAAS/Science Partner Journal)
records.claim_supported: Describes vacuum gauge calibration and measurement technology used to qualify hardware for China's space simulation and satellite programs; authors from the Science and Technology on Vacuum Technology and Physics Laboratory, Lanzhou Institute of Physics.
records.notes: Space: Science & Technology is an AAAS-affiliated Science Partner Journal (peer reviewed); DOI visible in URL structure. | Authors and institutional affiliation now confirmed via Crossref (were 'unknown'); geography CN confirmed directly rather than inferred.

records.title: Development and test of a rectangular CERN ConFlat-type flange
records.authors_or_org: Ana Miarnau Marin, Gerhard Schneider, Raymond Veness; Vacuum (Elsevier) — CERN
records.claim_supported: Reports design, manufacture, and leak/outgassing testing of a rectangular CERN-type ConFlat flange with copper gasket for the CERN Proton Synchrotron Beam Gas Ionisation instrument.
records.notes: Authors confirmed (CERN vacuum group); geography CH confirmed rather than inferred.

records.title: A comparison between the outgassing process of a titanium chamber and a stainless steel chamber
records.authors_or_org: Michio Minato, Yoshio Itoh; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Primary experimental comparison of outgassing rate/time behavior between titanium and stainless-steel UHV chamber materials.
records.notes: Authors confirmed (Japanese names); geography corrected from US placeholder to JP.

records.title: Reduction in Hydrogen Outgassing from Stainless Steels by a Medium-Temperature Heat Treatment
records.authors_or_org: C. D. Park, S. M. Chung, Xianghong Liu, Yulin Li; Journal of Vacuum Science & Technology A (AIP Publishing) — Cornell University (CESR)
records.claim_supported: A 30-hour, 250°C bakeout is reported to reduce stainless-steel hydrogen outgassing by a factor of over 70,000 versus untreated 300-series steel; electropolishing alone gives roughly a 30x reduction.
records.notes: Original ledger entry (year null, title paraphrased from a ResearchGate record, venue unconfirmed) now resolved to the actual published Cornell CESR paper. Title, authors, venue (JVST A), and year (2008) corrected; canonical URL switched from the ResearchGate mirror to the DOI resolver.

records.title: Reliability of turbomolecular vacuum pumps: A comparison of rolling element and magnetic bearing systems
records.authors_or_org: M. Heldner, H.-P. Kabelitz; Journal of Vacuum Science & Technology A (AIP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: Compares reliability, hydrocarbon contamination, and vibration between oil/grease-lubricated rolling-element and magnetic-bearing turbomolecular pump designs.
records.notes: Geography DE remains a tentative inference from German turbopump-industry authorship conventions (Kabelitz is linked to Leybold-era magnetic-bearing pump work per L07-013); not an independently confirmed affiliation.

records.title: Design aspects of a large scale turbomolecular pump with active magnetic bearings
records.authors_or_org: Bangcheng Han, Ziyuan Huang, Yun Le; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Presents engineering design tradeoffs for a large-diameter turbomolecular pump using active magnetic bearings instead of rolling-element bearings.
records.notes: Authors confirmed (Chinese magnetic-bearing turbopump research group, same lineage as L07-012); geography corrected from US placeholder to CN. Year shown as 2017 per Crossref print date; original 2016 reflects online-first date.

records.title: Stability control of high-speed magnetic levitation turbomolecular pumps with shock-excited disturbance
records.authors_or_org: Wei Shitong, Le Yun, Zhou Jinxiang, Yin Yifan, Zhang Di, Zheng Shiqiang; ISA Transactions (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Proposes a control scheme to maintain rotor stability of magnetically levitated turbomolecular pumps under shock disturbance (e.g., mechanical vibration/seismic events).
records.notes: Authors confirmed (shares 'Le Yun'/'Zheng Shiqiang' with L07-011, same Chinese magnetic-bearing pump research group); geography CN confirmed rather than a tentative guess.

records.title: Turbomolecular vacuum pumps with a new magnetic bearing concept
records.authors_or_org: H.-P. Kabelitz, J. K. Fremerey; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Early primary paper on a new magnetic-bearing turbomolecular pump concept, predating the widespread commercial adoption documented in L07-010.
records.notes: Authors confirmed; J. K. Fremerey was affiliated with KFA Julich, Germany, supporting the DE geography guess more strongly than before, though affiliation itself was not independently re-confirmed this session.

records.title: Vibration isolation with high thermal conductance for a cryogen-free dilution refrigerator
records.authors_or_org: Martin de Wit, Gesa Welker, Kier Heeck, Frank M. Buters, Hedwig J. Eerkens, Gert Koning, Harmen van der Meer, Dirk Bouwmeester, Tjerk H. Oosterkamp; Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: Presents a mechanical low-pass-filter vibration isolation design for a cryogen-free (pulse-tube-backed) dilution refrigerator operating at 10 mK, for scanning-probe use.
records.notes: Geography corrected from US placeholder to NL (Leiden University, Netherlands).

records.title: An ultra-low vibration cryostat with split design
records.authors_or_org: Jingxuan Zhang, Zhiyuan Wang, Qiang Wei, Jialu Chang, Qiyue Wu, Xiaoxu Chen, Wenhao Yuan, Ke Deng, Zehuang Lu, Jie Zhang; Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: A split-design, pulse-tube-cooled ultra-low-vibration cryostat achieves background vibration of 5x10^-7 m/s^2/Hz^1/2 at 1-10 Hz.
records.notes: Vibration figure in Crossref summary (5x10^-7 m/s^2/Hz^1/2) exactly matches the raw ledger's claim, confirming record identity. Geography CN confirmed.

records.title: Development of a cryogen-free dilution refrigerator with a cooling power of 2000 µW at around 100 mK
records.authors_or_org: Xiang Guan, De Ming Wang, Yong Jie Xie, Jie Fan, Zhong Qing Ji; Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP Publishing)
records.claim_supported: Reports a cryogen-free dilution refrigerator reaching a base temperature of 6.6 mK and 2000 µW of cooling power at ~102.5 mK, roughly an order of magnitude above conventional commercial units.
records.notes: Geography corrected from JP to CN: authors (including Zhong Qing Ji, Jie Fan, who also co-author a related 2022 Chinese Physics B cryogen-free DR paper) are Chinese, not Japanese as the raw ledger guessed.

records.title: Vibration-induced electrical noise in a cryogen-free dilution refrigerator: Characterization, mitigation, and impact on qubit coherence
records.authors_or_org: Rachpon Kalra, Arne Laucht, Juan Pablo Dehollain, Daniel Bar, Solomon Freer, Stephanie Simmons, Juha T. Muhonen, Andrea Morello; Review of Scientific Instruments (AIP Publishing) — University of New South Wales
records.claim_supported: Identifies triboelectric effects in wiring as the dominant vibration-induced electrical noise mechanism (5-10 kHz band) in cryogen-free dilution refrigerators and shows cable-jacketing mitigations reduce noise by over an order of magnitude, with direct relevance to superconducting-qubit coherence.
records.notes: Geography corrected from US to AU: the OSTI.GOV indexing in the raw ledger was a red herring for 'US DOE-funded origin' -- the actual authors are at the University of New South Wales, Australia (Andrea Morello's group). Canonical URL switched from the OSTI bibliographic record to the DOI resolver.

records.title: Thermal Performance of a 4 K High-frequency Pulse Tube Cryocooler with Different Working Fluids
records.authors_or_org: Zhaozhao Gao, Biao Yang, Xiaoyu Fan, Liubiao Chen, Junjie Wang; Journal of Thermal Science (Springer Nature)
records.claim_supported: Compares working-fluid options for 4 K high-frequency pulse-tube cryocoolers and their effect on cooling capacity/COP near liquid-helium temperature.
records.notes: DOI visible in URL. | Authors confirmed; geography CN confirmed.

records.title: Cooling performance improvement of a thermal-coupled two-stage high-frequency pulse tube cryocooler with hybrid Er3Ni/stainless steel screen regenerative material
records.authors_or_org: Bin Yang, Ziyao Liu, Min Gao, Jia Quan, Chenglong Liu, et al.; Applied Thermal Engineering (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Reports improved cooling capacity/relative COP for a two-stage high-frequency pulse-tube cryocooler using a hybrid Er3Ni/stainless-steel-screen regenerator material at ~20 K.
records.notes: Year corrected from 2024 (online-first, matching the ledger's PII-derived guess) to 2025 (Crossref primary publication year); authors confirmed, Chinese affiliation consistent with CN geography.
